danger_signals.visual_form: Worm character model degrades or shrinks as health decreases
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate threat to individual unit survival

danger_signals.visual_form: Circular cavities and deformations in terrain from weapon impacts
danger_signals.meaning: Active combat danger and terrain instability

danger_signals.visual_form: Water visible at base of map, darker color demarcation
danger_signals.meaning: Fatal boundary; worms falling into water die instantly

danger_signals.visual_form: Screen boundary or terrain cliff edge
danger_signals.meaning: Worms thrown off edges die; spatial danger zone

danger_signals.visual_form: Tombstone or grave symbol placed where worm dies
danger_signals.meaning: Permanent loss of unit; visual record of casualties

safety_signals.visual_form: Intact ground in themed environments (forest, beach, Martian, hell)
safety_signals.meaning: Safe positioning for worm placement and movement

safety_signals.visual_form: Reinforcement crate objects deployed on map
safety_signals.meaning: Resource availability; weapons and tools access points

safety_signals.visual_form: High ground positions, hills, platforms in generated landscape
safety_signals.meaning: Tactical advantage; reduced fall damage risk; defensive positioning

safety_signals.visual_form: Visual separation between worm teams across terrain
safety_signals.meaning: Temporary safety from direct engagement; opportunity for tactical repositioning

faction_indicators.color_identity: Typically red or distinct primary color per player
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Worm character model with team-specific palette
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Enemy teams rendered in contrasting colors (blue, green, yellow, etc.)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Team assignment shown in UI; grave markers colored per team

faction_indicators.color_identity: Distinct from player color; often blue or secondary palette
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Worm character with AI team coloring
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Visually differentiated from player units through palette swap
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Turn indicator shows which team controls current action

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Explosive barrels, land mines rendered in warning colors (typically red/yellow)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Environmental hazards displayed as shared threat across all teams

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Selected worm highlighted or animated; UI shows active player/team
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Worm responds to player input; can move, jump, deploy tools; time limit displayed

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Non-active worms appear static or in idle pose; UI dims or grays out player controls
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Worms do not respond to input; camera may pan to opponent's worm

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Explosion animation with particle effects; terrain deformation; screen shake possible
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Nearby worms react; knockback physics applied; health values update

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Visual indicator (UI message, screen border effect, or color tint change)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Accelerated turn timer; terrain may shrink or hazards increase; heightened urgency

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Multiple grave markers visible; fewer worms present on map; visual density decreases
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Remaining worms become more cautious or aggressive; fewer movement options due to terrain loss

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Final worm of losing team explodes with grave marker; winning team worms celebrate or idle
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Game halts; victory screen displays; surviving worms may perform emotes or animations

environmental_storytelling.example: Forest terrain (trees, organic shapes), Martian terrain (alien landscape), Beach (sand, water), Hell (volcanic/hellish elements) — each conveys setting without narrative exposition
As weapons detonate, the landscape becomes increasingly scarred and fragmented, visually narrating the escalation of conflict and environmental devastation
Tombstones left by fallen worms create a visual history of the battle; crowded graveyards indicate lengthy, brutal engagements
Explosive barrels, land mines positioned in terrain suggest dangerous zones; their presence communicates high-risk areas without explicit warning text
Supply crates scattered across map indicate resource scarcity and competition; placement influences tactical narrative of the battle

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Stationary pose, awaiting input; may blink or shift weight to show life
Highlighted or glowing effect; responds to movement commands (walk, jump, tool use)
Visual damage indicators; reduced movement speed or altered animation; knockback response to explosions
Death animation; self-explosion effect; leaves grave marker; removed from play
Stationary; different color palette; not responsive to current player input
Knockback physics applied; recoil animation; health value visibly decreases
Stable geometry; worms walk/stand without sliding; provides solid movement platform
Deforms into crater-like cavity; becomes unstable; may cause worms to fall or slide toward water
Visible on terrain; can be collected by worm contact; disappears upon pickup
Sits passively on terrain; triggers explosion if worm contacts or weapon detonates nearby; poses shared threat

notes: Worms (1995) uses minimal explicit narrative to communicate world-state. Instead, it relies heavily on visual design language: team color differentiation, environmental theming, terrain deformation, and symbolic markers (graves, crates, hazards) to convey game state and danger. The procedurally generated levels using alpha-numeric seeds ensure varied but recognizable environmental themes, reinforcing tactical readiness through familiar visual patterns. Danger signals are primarily spatial (edges, water) and consequence-based (graves, health depletion). The game communicates political/faction affiliation through pure color and palette—no logos, sigils, or dialogue present. This minimalist approach was typical of 1995 strategy games and influenced how subsequent Worms titles expanded faction identity.
